samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal circumstances, the traffic light should cycle through green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it should be shortened to 10 clocks. If the remaining green time is less than 10 clocks, it should remain unchanged.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button input.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <enum id="idle">Idle state.</enum>
        <enum id="s1_red">State 1 - Red.</enum>
        <enum id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow.</enum>
        <enum id="s3_green">State 3 - Green.</enum>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>State transition logic.</description>
            <case>
                <item value="idle">Transition to s1_red.</item>
                <item value="s1_red">Transition to s3_green after a count of 60.</item>
                <item value="s2_yellow">Transition to s1_red after a count of 5.</item>
                <item value="s3_green">Transition to s2_yellow after a count of 10.</item>
            </case>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <description>Counter logic.</description>
            <if>
                <condition value="!rst_n">Set cnt to 10.</condition>
                <condition value="pass_request && green">
                    <description>Shorten green time to 10 clocks.</description>
                    <then>Set cnt to 10.</then>
                </condition>
                <condition value="!green && p_green">Set cnt to 60.</condition>
                <condition value="!yellow && p_yellow">Set cnt to 5.</condition>
                <condition value="!red && p_red">Set cnt to 10.</condition>
                <else>
                    <description>Decrement cnt by 1.</description>
                    <then>Set cnt to cnt - 1.</then>
                </else>
            </if>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. Here's a summary of each state:
            idle: Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to s1_red state occurs immediately.
            s1_red: Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 60 before transitioning to s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in s1_red state.
            s2_yellow: Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 5 before transitioning to s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in s2_yellow state.
            s3_green: Sets the green signal to 1 and waits for a count of 10 before transitioning to s2_yellow state. Otherwise, it remains in s3_green state.
            The second always block handles the counting logic of the internal counter (cnt). The counter is decremented by 1 on every positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal. The counter values are adjusted based on various conditions:
            If (!rst_n), the counter is set to 10.
            If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10.
            If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60.
            If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5.
            If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10.
            Otherwise, the counter is decremented normally.
            The assign statement assigns the value of the internal counter (cnt) to the output clock.
            The final always block handles the output signals. It assigns the previous values (p_red, p_yellow, p_green) to the output signals (red, yellow, green) on the positive edge of the clock or negative edge of the reset signal.
        </description>
        <description>Assign cnt to output clock.</description>
        <assign>
            <lhs>clock</lhs>
            <rhs>cnt</rhs>
        </assign>
    </implementation>
</module>
